transliteration:
yāvat sañjāyate kiñcit sattvaṃ sthāvara-jaṅgamam |
kṣetra-kṣetrajña-saṃyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣabha ||
literal_meaning: Whatever comes into being, whether moving or unmoving, know it to be born from the union of the field (body/mind) and the knower of the field (consciousness).
contextual_meaning: Every perception, emotion, or thought is a momentary marriage between the observing self and the observable world; without this inner–outer handshake, nothing ‘exists’ for us psychologically.
psychological_state: Meta-awareness: the client realizes that anxiety, trees, and smartphones alike are co-created by attention meeting phenomena—nothing is ‘out there’ independent of the observer.
trigger_scenario: A person in therapy suddenly sees their panic attack as the intersection of bodily sensations (field) and the observing mind (knower); the spell breaks, and the attack loses its grip.
anchor_text: Every experience is born when awareness meets the world.
